title: ouratea recurva
intro: Ouratea recurva, also known as the 'Red Ironwood', is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, and its bark and root are used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating infections. Ouratea recurva can be prepared as a decoction or infusion for herbal remedies, and its extracts are also used in modern medicine for their potential antiviral and antioxidant effects.
